mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 250 in CPIAUCSL triggers central bank tightening, increasing discount rates and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 250, or inflation-adjusted earnings growth outperforms bond yields.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) signals robust economic demand, driving corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no significant correlation with combined GDPC1/INDPRO growth, or individual series exhibit negative Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 4% during high labor force participation signals tight labor markets, pushing wage inflation and prompting Fed rate hikes that depress SPX returns over 5 days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 5d returns in 70%+ of instances where UNRATE < 4% and PAYEMS > 150k, contradicting the predicted negative relationship.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) indicates weak supply chain capacity, reducing corporate margins and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with INDPRO declines, or manufacturing PMI data contradicts the negative relationship.

mechanism_narrative: A 10%+ drop in housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening demand for construction, reducing related sector earnings and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 5d returns in 60%+ of instances with HOUST declines > 10%, or housing affordability indices contradict the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL indicate inflationary pressures forcing rate hikes, creating a negative environment for equity valuations and producing 20d SPX declines.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where both FEDFUNDS > 2% and CPIAUCSL > 245, or real rates remain negative.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer spending (RSAFS) combined with stable GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates strong demand-side momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no significant correlation with combined RSAFS/GDPC1 trends, or retail sales data contradicts the positive relationship.
